verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचस इत्थं द्विजमुख्येन सह सङ्कथयन् हरि: ।सर्वभूतमनोऽभिज्ञ: स्मयमान उवाच तम् ॥ १ ॥ब्रह्मण्यो ब्राह्मणं कृष्णो भगवान् प्रहसन् प्रियम् ।प्रेम्णा निरीक्षणेनैव प्रेक्षन् खलु सतां गति: ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācasa itthaṁ dvija-mukhyenasaha saṅkathayan hariḥsarva-bhūta-mano-’bhijñaḥsmayamāna uvāca tam
verses.text: [Śukadeva Gosvāmī said:] Lord Hari, Kṛṣṇa, perfectly knows the hearts of all living beings, and He is especially devoted to the brāhmaṇas. While the Supreme Lord, the goal of all saintly persons, conversed in this way with the best of the twice-born, He laughed and spoke the following words to that dear friend of His, the brāhmaṇa Sudāmā, all the while smiling and looking upon him with affection.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाचकिमुपायनमानीतं ब्रह्मन् मे भवता गृहात् ।अण्वप्युपाहृतं भक्तै: प्रेम्णा भूर्येव मे भवेत् ।भूर्यप्यभक्तोपहृतं न मे तोषाय कल्पते ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvācakim upāyanam ānītaṁbrahman me bhavatā gṛhātaṇv apy upāhṛtaṁ bhaktaiḥpremṇā bhury eva me bhavetbhūry apy abhaktopahṛtaṁna me toṣāya kalpate
verses.text: The Supreme Lord said: O brāhmaṇa, what gift have you brought Me from home? I regard as great even the smallest gift offered by My devotees in pure love, but even great offerings presented by nondevotees do not please Me.

verses.sanskrit: पत्रं पुष्पं फलं तोयं यो मे भक्त्या प्रयच्छति ।तदहं भक्त्युपहृतमश्न‍ामि प्रयतात्मन: ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: patraṁ puṣpaṁ phalaṁ toyaṁyo me bhaktyā prayacchatitad ahaṁ bhakty-upahṛtamaśnāmi prayatātmanaḥ
verses.text: If one offers Me with love and devotion a leaf, a flower, a fruit or water, I will accept it.

verses.sanskrit: इत्युक्तोऽपि द्वियस्तस्मै व्रीडित: पतये श्रिय: ।पृथुकप्रसृतिं राजन् न प्रायच्छदवाङ्‍मुख: ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: ity ukto ’pi dviyas tasmaivrīḍitaḥ pataye śriyaḥpṛthuka-prasṛtiṁ rājanna prāyacchad avāṅ-mukhaḥ
verses.text: [Śukadeva Gosvāmī continued:] Even after being addressed in this way, O King, the brāhmaṇa felt too embarrassed to offer his palmfuls of flat rice to the husband of the goddess of fortune. He simply kept his head bowed in shame.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वभूतात्मद‍ृक् साक्षात् तस्यागमनकारणम् ।विज्ञायाचिन्तयन्नायं श्रीकामो माभजत्पुरा ॥ ६ ॥पत्न्‍या: पतिव्रतायास्तु सखा प्रियचिकीर्षया ।प्राप्तो मामस्य दास्यामि सम्पदोऽमर्त्यदुर्लभा: ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: sarva-bhūtātma-dṛk sākṣāttasyāgamana-kāraṇamvijṅāyācintayan nāyaṁśrī-kāmo mābhajat purā
verses.text: Being the direct witness in the hearts of all living beings, Lord Kṛṣṇa fully understood why Sudāmā had come to see Him. Thus He thought, “In the past My friend has never worshiped Me out of a desire for material opulence, but now he comes to Me to satisfy his chaste and devoted wife. I will give him riches that even the immortal demigods cannot obtain.”

verses.sanskrit: इत्थं विचिन्त्य वसनाच्चीरबद्धान्द्विजन्मन: ।स्वयं जहार किमिदमिति पृथुकतण्डुलान् ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: itthaṁ vicintya vasanāccīra-baddhān dvi-janmanaḥsvayaṁ jahāra kim idamiti pṛthuka-taṇḍulān
verses.text: Thinking like this, the Lord snatched from the brāhmaṇa’s garment the grains of flat rice tied up in an old piece of cloth and exclaimed, “What is this?

verses.sanskrit: नन्वेतदुपनीतं मे परमप्रीणनं सखे ।तर्पयन्त्यङ्ग मां विश्वमेते पृथुकतण्डुला: ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: nanv etad upanītaṁ meparama-prīṇanaṁ sakhetarpayanty aṅga māṁ viśvamete pṛthuka-taṇḍulāḥ
verses.text: “My friend, have You brought this for Me? It gives Me extreme pleasure. Indeed, these few grains of flat rice will satisfy not only Me but also the entire universe.”

verses.sanskrit: इति मुष्टिं सकृज्जग्ध्वा द्वितीयां जग्धुमाददे ।तावच्छ्रीर्जगृहे हस्तं तत्परा परमेष्ठिन: ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: iti muṣṭiṁ sakṛj jagdhvādvitīyāṁ jagdhum ādadetāvac chrīr jagṛhe hastaṁtat-parā parameṣṭhinaḥ
verses.text: After saying this, the Supreme Lord ate one palmful and was about to eat a second when the devoted goddess Rukmiṇī took hold of His hand.

verses.sanskrit: एतावतालं विश्वात्मन् सर्वसम्पत्समृद्धये ।अस्मिन्लोकेऽथवामुष्मिन् पुंसस्त्वत्तोषकारणम् ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: etāvatālaṁ viśvātmansarva-sampat-samṛddhayeasmin loke ’tha vāmuṣminpuṁsas tvat-toṣa-kāraṇam
verses.text: [Queen Rukmiṇī said:] This is more than enough, O Soul of the universe, to secure him an abundance of all kinds of wealth in this world and the next. After all, one’s prosperity depends simply on Your satisfaction.

verses.sanskrit: ब्राह्मणस्तां तु रजनीमुषित्वाच्युतमन्दिरे ।भुक्त्वा पीत्वा सुखं मेने आत्मानं स्वर्गतं यथा ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: brāhmaṇas tāṁ tu rajanīmuṣitvācyuta-mandirebhuktvā pītvā sukhaṁ meneātmānaṁ svar-gataṁ yathā
verses.text: [Śukadeva Gosvāmī continued:] The brāhmaṇa spent that night in Lord Acyuta’s palace after eating and drinking to his full satisfaction. He felt as if he had gone to the spiritual world.

verses.sanskrit: श्वोभूते विश्वभावेन स्वसुखेनाभिवन्दित: ।जगाम स्वालयं तात पथ्यनुव्रज्य नन्दित: ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: śvo-bhūte viśva-bhāvenasva-sukhenābhivanditaḥjagāma svālayaṁ tātapathy anavrajya nanditaḥ
verses.text: The next day, Sudāmā set off for home while being honored by Lord Kṛṣṇa, the self-satisfied maintainer of the universe. The brāhmaṇa felt greatly delighted, my dear King, as he walked along the road.

verses.sanskrit: स चालब्ध्वा धनं कृष्णान्न तु याचितवान्स्वयम् ।स्वगृहान् व्रीडितोऽगच्छन्महद्दर्शननिर्वृत: ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa cālabdhvā dhanaṁ kṛṣṇānna tu yācitavān svayamsva-gṛhān vrīḍito ’gacchanmahad-darśana-nirvṛtaḥ
verses.text: Although he had apparently received no wealth from Lord Kṛṣṇa, Sudāmā was too shy to beg for it on his own. He simply returned home, feeling perfectly satisfied to have had the Supreme Lord’s audience.

verses.sanskrit: अहो ब्रह्मण्यदेवस्य द‍ृष्टा ब्रह्मण्यता मया ।यद् दरिद्रतमो लक्ष्मीमाश्लिष्टो बिभ्रतोरसि ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: aho brahmaṇya-devasyadṛṣṭā brahmaṇyatā mayāyad daridratamo lakṣmīmāśliṣṭo bibhratorasi
verses.text: [Sudāmā thought:] Lord Kṛṣṇa is known to be devoted to the brāhmaṇas, and now I have personally seen this devotion. Indeed, He who carries the goddess of fortune on His chest has embraced the poorest beggar.

verses.sanskrit: क्व‍ाहं दरिद्र: पापीयान् क्व‍ कृष्ण: श्रीनिकेतन: ।ब्रह्मबन्धुरिति स्माहं बाहुभ्यां परिरम्भित: ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: kvāhaṁ daridraḥ pāpīyānkva kṛṣṇaḥ śrī-niketanaḥbrahma-bandhur iti smāhaṁbāhubhyāṁ parirambhitaḥ
verses.text: Who am I? A sinful, poor friend of a brāhmaṇa. And who is Kṛṣṇa? The Supreme Personality of Godhead, full in six opulences. Nonetheless, He has embraced me with His two arms.

verses.sanskrit: निवासित: प्रियाजुष्टे पर्यङ्के भ्रातरो यथा ।महिष्या वीजित: श्रान्तो बालव्यजनहस्तया ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: nivāsitaḥ priyā-juṣṭeparyaṅke bhrātaro yathāmahiṣyā vījitaḥ śrāntobāla-vyajana-hastayā
verses.text: He treated me just like one of His brothers, making me sit on the bed of His beloved consort. And because I was fatigued, His queen personally fanned me with a yak-tail cāmara.

verses.sanskrit: शुश्रूषया परमया पादसंवाहनादिभि: ।पूजितो देवदेवेन विप्रदेवेन देववत् ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: śuśrūṣayā paramayāpāda-saṁvāhanādibhiḥpūjito deva-devenavipra-devena deva-vat
verses.text: Although He is the Lord of all demigods and the object of worship for all brāhmaṇas, He worshiped me as if I were a demigod myself, massaging my feet and rendering other humble services.

verses.sanskrit: स्वर्गापवर्गयो: पुंसां रसायां भुवि सम्पदाम् ।सर्वासामपि सिद्धीनां मूलं तच्चरणार्चनम् ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: svargāpavargayoḥ puṁsāṁrasāyāṁ bhuvi sampadāmsarvāsām api siddhīnāṁmūlaṁ tac-caraṇārcanam
verses.text: Devotional service to His lotus feet is the root cause of all the perfections a person can find in heaven, in liberation, in the subterranean regions and on earth.

verses.sanskrit: अधनोऽयं धनं प्राप्य माद्यन्नुच्चैर्न मां स्मरेत् ।इति कारुणिको नूनं धनं मेऽभूरि नाददात् ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: adhano ’yaṁ dhanaṁ prāpyamādyann uccair na māṁ smaretiti kāruṇiko nūnaṁdhanaṁ me ’bhūri nādadāt
verses.text: Thinking “If this poor wretch suddenly becomes rich, he will forget Me in his intoxicating happiness,” the compassionate Lord did not grant me even a little wealth.

verses.sanskrit: इति तच्चिन्तयन्नन्त: प्राप्तो निजगृहान्तिकम् ।सूर्यानलेन्दुसङ्काशैर्विमानै: सर्वतो वृतम् ॥ २१ ॥विचित्रोपवनोद्यानै: कूजद्‌द्विजकुलाकुलै: ।प्रोत्फुल्ल‍कमुदाम्भोजकह्लारोत्पलवारिभि: ॥ २२ ॥जुष्टं स्वलङ्कृतै: पुम्भि: स्‍त्रीभिश्च हरिणाक्षिभि: ।किमिदं कस्य वा स्थानं कथं तदिदमित्यभूत् ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: iti tac cintayann antaḥprāpto niya-gṛhāntikamsūryānalendu-saṅkāśairvimānaiḥ sarvato vṛtam
verses.text: [Śukadeva Gosvāmī continued:] Thinking thus to himself, Sudāmā finally came to the place where his home stood. But that place was now crowded on all sides with towering, celestial palaces rivaling the combined brilliance of the sun, fire and the moon. There were splendorous courtyards and gardens, each filled with flocks of cooing birds and beautified by ponds in which kumuda, ambhoja, kahlāra and utpala lotuses grew. Finely attired men and doe-eyed women stood in attendance. Sudāmā wondered, “What is all this? Whose property is it? How has this all come about?”

verses.sanskrit: एवं मीमांसमानं तं नरा नार्योऽमरप्रभा: ।प्रत्यगृह्णन् महाभागं गीतवाद्येन भूयसा ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁ mīmāṁsamānaṁ taṁnarā nāryo ’mara-prabhāḥpratyagṛhṇan mahā-bhāgaṁgīta-vādyena bhūyasā
verses.text: As he continued to ponder in this way, the beautiful men — and maidservants, as effulgent as demigods, came forward to greet their greatly fortunate master with loud song and instrumental music.

verses.sanskrit: पतिमागतमाकर्ण्य पत्न्‍युद्धर्षातिसम्भ्रमा ।निश्चक्राम गृहात्तूर्णं रूपिणी श्रीरिवालयात् ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: patim āgatam ākarṇyapatny uddharṣāti-sambhramāniścakrāma gṛhāt tūrṇaṁrūpiṇī śrīr ivālayāt
verses.text: When she heard that her husband had arrived, the brāhmaṇa’s wife quickly came out of the house in a jubilant flurry. She resembled the goddess of fortune herself emerging from her divine abode.

verses.sanskrit: पतिव्रता पतिं द‍ृष्ट्वा प्रेमोत्कण्ठाश्रुलोचना ।मीलिताक्ष्यनमद्बुद्ध्या मनसा परिषस्वजे ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: pati-vratā patiṁ dṛṣṭvāpremotkaṇṭhāśru-locanāmīlitākṣy anamad buddhyāmanasā pariṣasvaje
verses.text: When the chaste lady saw her husband, her eyes filled with tears of love and eagerness. As she held her eyes closed, she solemnly bowed down to him, and in her heart she embraced him.

verses.sanskrit: पत्नीं वीक्ष्य विस्फुरन्तीं देवीं वैमानिकीमिव ।दासीनां निष्ककण्ठीनां मध्ये भान्तीं स विस्मित: ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: patnīṁ vīkṣya visphurantīṁdevīṁ vaimānikīm ivadāsīnāṁ niṣka-kaṇṭhīnāṁmadhye bhāntīṁ sa vismitaḥ
verses.text: Sudāmā was amazed to see his wife. Shining forth in the midst of maidservants adorned with jeweled lockets, she looked as effulgent as a demigoddess in her celestial airplane.

verses.sanskrit: प्रीत: स्वयं तया युक्त: प्रविष्टो निजमन्दिरम् ।मणिस्तम्भशतोपेतं महेन्द्रभवनं यथा ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: prītaḥ svayaṁ tayā yuktaḥpraviṣṭo nija-mandirammaṇi-stambha-śatopetaṁmahendra-bhavanaṁ yathā
verses.text: With pleasure he took his wife with him and entered his house, where there were hundreds of gem-studded pillars, just as in the palace of Lord Mahendra.

verses.sanskrit: पय:फेननिभा: शय्या दान्ता रुक्‍मपरिच्छदा: ।पर्यङ्का हेमदण्डानि चामरव्यजनानि च ॥ २९ ॥आसनानि च हैमानि मृदूपस्तरणानि च ।मुक्तादामविलम्बीनि वितानानि द्युमन्ति च ॥ ३० ॥स्वच्छस्फटिककुड्येषु महामारकतेषु च ।रत्नदीपान् भ्राजमानान् ललनारत्नसंयुता: ॥ ३१ ॥विलोक्य ब्राह्मणस्तत्र समृद्धी: सर्वसम्पदाम् ।तर्कयामास निर्व्यग्र: स्वसमृद्धिमहैतुकीम् ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: payaḥ-phena-nibhāḥ śayyādāntā rukma-paricchadāḥparyaṅkā hema-daṇḍānicāmara-vyajanāni ca
verses.text: In Sudāmā’s home were beds as soft and white as the foam of milk, with bedsteads made of ivory and ornamented with gold. There were also couches with golden legs, as well as royal cāmara fans, golden thrones, soft cushions and gleaming canopies hung with strings of pearls. Upon the walls of sparkling crystal glass, inlaid with precious emeralds, shone jeweled lamps, and the women in the palace were all adorned with precious gems. As he viewed this luxurious opulence of all varieties, the brāhmaṇa calmly reasoned to himself about his unexpected prosperity.

verses.sanskrit: नूनं बतैतन्मम दुर्भगस्यशश्वद्दरिद्रस्य समृद्धिहेतु: ।महाविभूतेरवलोकतोऽन्योनैवोपपद्येत यदूत्तमस्य ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: nūnaṁ bataitan mama durbhagasyaśaśvad daridrasya samṛddhi-hetuḥmahā-vibhūter avalokato ’nyonaivopapadyeta yadūttamasya
verses.text: [Sudāmā thought:] I have always been poor. Certainly the only possible way that such an unfortunate person as myself could become suddenly rich is that Lord Kṛṣṇa, the supremely opulent chief of the Yadu dynasty, has glanced upon Me.

verses.sanskrit: नन्वब्रुवाणो दिशते समक्षंयाचिष्णवे भूर्यपि भूरिभोज: ।पर्जन्यवत्तत् स्वयमीक्षमाणोदाशार्हकाणामृषभ: सखा मे ॥ ३४ ॥
verses.transliteration: nanv abruvāṇo diśate samakṣaṁyāciṣṇave bhūry api bhūri-bhojaḥparjanya-vat tat svayam īkṣamāṇodāśārhakāṇām ṛṣabhaḥ sakhā me
verses.text: After all, my friend Kṛṣṇa, the most exalted of the Dāśārhas and the enjoyer of unlimited wealth, noticed that I secretly intended to beg from Him. Thus even though He said nothing about it when I stood before Him, He actually bestowed upon me the most abundant riches. In this way He acted just like a merciful rain cloud.

verses.sanskrit: किञ्चित्करोत्युर्वपि यत् स्वदत्तंसुहृत्कृतं फल्ग्वपि भूरिकारी ।मयोपनीतं पृथुकैकमुष्टिंप्रत्यग्रहीत् प्रीतियुतो महात्मा ॥ ३५ ॥
verses.transliteration: kiñcit karoty urv api yat sva-dattaṁsuhṛt-kṛtaṁ phalgv api bhūri-kārīmayopaṇītaṁ pṛthukaika-muṣṭiṁpratyagrahīt prīti-yuto mahātmā
verses.text: The Lord considers even His greatest benedictions to be insignificant, while He magnifies even a small service rendered to Him by His well-wishing devotee. Thus with pleasure the Supreme Soul accepted a single palmful of the flat rice I brought Him.

verses.sanskrit: तस्यैव मे सौहृदसख्यमैत्रीदास्यं पुनर्जन्मनि जन्मनि स्यात् ।महानुभावेन गुणालयेनविषज्जतस्तत्पुरुषप्रसङ्ग: ॥ ३६ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasyaiva me sauhṛda-sakhya-maitrī-dāsyaṁ punar janmani janmani syātmahānubhāvena guṇālayenaviṣajjatas tat-puruṣa-prasaṅgaḥ
verses.text: The Lord is the supremely compassionate reservoir of all transcendental qualities. Life after life may I serve Him with love, friendship and sympathy, and may I cultivate such firm attachment for Him by the precious association of His devotees.

verses.sanskrit: भक्ताय चित्रा भगवान् हि सम्पदोराज्यं विभूतीर्न समर्थयत्यज: ।अदीर्घबोधाय विचक्षण: स्वयंपश्यन् निपातं धनिनां मदोद्भ‍वम् ॥ ३७ ॥
verses.transliteration: bhaktāya citrā bhagavān hi sampadorājyaṁ vibhūtīr na samarthayaty ajaḥadīrgha-bodhāya vicakṣaṇaḥ svayaṁpaśyan nipātaṁ dhanināṁ madodbhavam
verses.text: To a devotee who lacks spiritual insight, the Supreme Lord will not grant the wonderful opulences of this world — kingly power and material assets. Indeed, in His infinite wisdom the unborn Lord well knows how the intoxication of pride can cause the downfall of the wealthy.

verses.sanskrit: इत्थं व्यवसितो बुद्ध्या भक्तोऽतीव जनार्दने ।विषयान् जायया त्यक्ष्यन्बुभुजे नातिलम्पट: ॥ ३८ ॥
verses.transliteration: itthaṁ vyavasito buddhyābhakto ’tīva janārdaneviṣayān jāyayā tyakṣyanbubhuje nāti-lampaṭaḥ
verses.text: [Śukadeva Gosvāmī continued:] Thus firmly fixing his determination by means of his spiritual intelligence, Sudāmā remained absolutely devoted to Lord Kṛṣṇa, the shelter of all living beings. Free from avarice, he enjoyed, together with his wife, the sense pleasures that had been bestowed upon him, always with the idea of eventually renouncing all sense gratification.

verses.sanskrit: तस्य वै देवदेवस्य हरेर्यज्ञपते: प्रभो: ।ब्राह्मणा: प्रभवो दैवं न तेभ्यो विद्यते परम् ॥ ३९ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasya vai deva-devasyaharer yajña-pateḥ prabhoḥbrāhmaṇāḥ prabhavo daivaṁna tebhyo vidyate param
verses.text: Lord Hari is the God of all gods, the master of all sacrifices, and the supreme ruler. But He accepts the saintly brāhmaṇas as His masters, and so there exists no deity higher than them.

verses.sanskrit: एवं स विप्रो भगवत्सुहृत्तदाद‍ृष्ट्वा स्वभृत्यैरजितं पराजितम् ।तद्ध्यानवेगोद्ग्रथितात्मबन्धन-स्तद्धाम लेभेऽचिरत: सतां गतिम् ॥ ४० ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁ sa vipro bhagavat-suhṛt tadādṛṣṭvā sva-bhṛtyair ajitaṁ parājitamtad-dhyāna-vegodgrathitātma-bandhanastad-dhāma lebhe ’cirataḥ satāṁ gatim
verses.text: Thus seeing how the unconquerable Supreme Lord is nonetheless conquered by His own servants, the Lord’s dear brāhmaṇa friend felt the remaining knots of material attachment within his heart being cut by the force of his constant meditation on the Lord. In a short time he attained Lord Kṛṣṇa’s supreme abode, the destination of great saints.

verses.sanskrit: एतद् ब्रह्मण्यदेवस्य श्रुत्वा ब्रह्मण्यतां नर: ।लब्धभावो भगवति कर्मबन्धाद् विमुच्यते ॥ ४१ ॥
verses.transliteration: etad brahmaṇya-devasyaśrutvā brahmaṇyatāṁ naraḥlabdha-bhāvo bhagavatikarma-bandhād vimucyate
verses.text: The Lord always shows brāhmaṇas special favor. Anyone who hears this account of the Supreme Lord’s kindness to brāhmaṇas will come to develop love for the Lord and thus become freed from the bondage of material work.
